label: Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding

node.label: Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding
node.definition: Speech-recognition systems transform acoustic signals into text, timestamps, speakers, intents, or semantic representations under varied noise and accents.
node.why_it_matters: This system is an application boundary where technical capability, evidence, safety controls, economics, and user workflow must succeed together.
examples: workflows combining Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding with Encoder–decoder transformers
workflows combining Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding with Waveforms, audio & spectrograms
workflows combining Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding with Large-scale pretraining
limitations: End-to-end quality is bounded by the weakest data, model, tool, interface, policy, infrastructure, and human-oversight component. Its direct dependencies include Encoder–decoder transformers, Waveforms, audio & spectrograms.

dependencies.rationale: Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding integrates Encoder–decoder transformers as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding integrates Waveforms, audio & spectrograms as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding integrates Large-scale pretraining as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
In this atlas graph, Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding has an `applies` dependency on Model servers & inference runtimes with `common` strength.
Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding integrates Beam search & constrained decoding as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding integrates Encoder-only bidirectional transformers as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
